metadata.title: Filosofía: 道德经 (Dao De Jing) - Capítulo 1
metadata.description: The foundational opening text of Laozi's Dao De Jing, exploring the nameless and the named.

sentences.original: 道可道，非常道。名可名，非常名。
sentences.translation: The Way that can be spoken of is not the eternal Way; The name that can be named is not the eternal name.

chunks.meaning: The Way that can be spoken of,
The name that can be named,

sentences.original: 无名，天地之始。有名，万物之母。
sentences.translation: The nameless is the origin of heaven and earth; The named is the mother of all things.

chunks.meaning: is the beginning of heaven and earth.
is the mother of the ten thousand things (all things).

sentences.original: 故常无，欲以观其妙。常有，欲以观其徼。
sentences.translation: Therefore, constantly without desire, one observes its wonders; Constantly with desire, one observes its manifestations.

chunks.meaning: one wishes to observe its mystery/wonders.
one wishes to observe its boundaries/manifestations.

sentences.original: 此两者同出而异名，同谓之玄。玄之又玄，众妙之门。
sentences.translation: These two emerge from the same source but have different names; together they are called the ultimate mystery. Mystery of mysteries, the gateway to all wonders.

sentences.original: 这是一条正道。 (Zhè shì yī tiáo zhèng dào.)
日常生活需要规律。 (Rìcháng shēnghuó xūyào guīlǜ.)
千里之行，始于足下。 (Qiānlǐ zhī xíng, shǐ yú zú xià.)
sentences.translation: A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.

sentences.original: 这个办法太妙了。 (Zhège bànfǎ tài miào le.)
这个道理非常玄奥。 (Zhège dàolǐ fēicháng xuán'ào.)
sentences.translation: This truth is incredibly profound and mysterious.
